profile.name: Md. Tanzid Haque
profile.title: Software Developer
profile.tagline: Full-stack engineer building scalable SaaS, ERP, and e-commerce systems with Laravel, React, and Next.js.
profile.summary: Full Stack Software Developer with 2+ years of experience building production-grade systems. Strong focus on performance optimization, clean architecture, and rapid product delivery. Experienced in modern AI-assisted development workflows — leveraging automation and developer tooling to ship faster while maintaining high-quality codebases.

highlights: Designed and implemented scalable authentication systems using Sanctum and JWT.
Optimized complex database queries, reducing response times from 3s to 500ms.
Integrated secure payment gateways including SSLCOMMERZ and Stripe.
Built real-time systems using Laravel Reverb, Pusher, SMS, and email pipelines.
Implemented queue-based architecture with Laravel Horizon for background jobs.
Developed automated reporting systems (PDF, CSV, Excel exports).
Engineered presigned upload pipelines using Cloudflare R2.
Managed production infrastructure, CI/CD pipelines, and VPS deployments.
Developed ERP modules with role-based access control and scalable architecture.
Built high-performance UI components handling datasets of 100k+ records.
Designed analytics dashboards for distributors and operational teams.
Implemented real-time updates and reporting systems.
Built internal chat system using Laravel Reverb.
Contributed to ERP system development with modular and reusable components.
Improved frontend performance and usability for large-scale data interfaces.
Assisted in reporting and dashboard feature development.

projects.name: PingPanda
projects.subtitle: SaaS Website Monitoring Platform
highlights: Real-time monitoring platform for uptime, API health, and event tracking.
Secure API key system with request validation and rate control.
Webhook ingestion system for external integrations.
Discord bot integration for instant alerting and incident notifications.
Optimized data fetching and caching with Prisma and TanStack Query.

projects.name: PDF Craft
projects.subtitle: AI-Powered PDF Chat SaaS
highlights: AI-powered document chat using semantic search and embeddings.
Vector-based retrieval pipeline for accurate contextual responses.
Subscription billing and access control with Stripe.
Optimized large document parsing and rendering pipelines.
Secure API layer with usage limits and protected endpoints.

projects.name: Big Data Import / Export
projects.subtitle: High-volume data pipeline system
highlights: High-volume CSV import/export pipelines for large datasets.
Asynchronous job processing using Celery and Redis.
Automation scripts for data ingestion, cleanup, and validation.
Secure reporting delivery system with email integration and job tracking.

writing.title: Permission Architecture: RBAC in Scalable Systems
writing.summary: Designed and explained scalable role-based access control for SaaS applications — covering structure, trade-offs, and implementation patterns.

writing.title: Async Processing with Queues & Laravel Horizon
writing.summary: Explored queue-based architecture for handling background jobs — improving system performance and reliability in production environments.

writing.title: Load Balancing Strategies for High-Traffic Systems
writing.summary: Analyzed L4/L7 load balancing techniques, algorithms, and scaling patterns — and their role in building resilient distributed systems.

principles.title: Clean architecture
principles.body: Code that other people can read, extend, and trust. Modular boundaries over clever abstractions.

principles.title: Performance as a feature
principles.body: Profile first, optimize what matters. Reduced a 3-second query to 500ms by reading the right indexes, not by adding cache.

principles.title: Ship, then iterate
principles.body: Product-oriented thinking — balance speed, quality, and user impact. Get something real in front of users early.

principles.title: AI as leverage
principles.body: Modern dev workflows treat AI as a power tool — for codegen, review, and exploration — not a replacement for engineering judgment.

education.degree: B.Sc. in Electrical and Electronic Engineering
